codeReview.summary: The fix is a targeted one-line `bun.lock` cleanup that removes the stale `drizzle-orm` direct dependency from the `packages/api` workspace block. That change now matches the earlier manifest/package-boundary cleanup: `packages/api/package.json` no longer declares `drizzle-orm`, the current lockfile no longer records it under `packages/api`, and the legitimate `packages/db` dependency remains present.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature-level verification step for this fix assumes an older `bun.lock` text shape and does not match the current lockfile format, so workers had to substitute a custom check to verify the cleaned `packages/api` block.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission features.json:365 uses `text.index('packages/api:')`, but the real lockfile uses quoted workspace keys at bun.lock:159 (`"packages/api":`). The worker handoff at /home/louis/.factory/missions/bf937643-326f-44d3-a49d-533fd744ae00/handoffs/2026-04-04T01-00-30-429Z__preflight-lockfile-manifest-sync__352a5810-5915-479d-a10b-86f9284832e9.json also records this mismatch and the corrected verification approach.

summary: Reviewed the rerun fix against the original failure. Commit `f63a0aa` removes the exact stale `drizzle-orm` entry that kept `bun.lock` out of sync with `packages/api/package.json`, while preserving the valid `packages/db` dependency, so it adequately resolves the prior blocking scrutiny finding.
